description_long: This factory building features a sprawling, rectangular layout with multiple interconnected structures, including brick and metal-clad sections. The roofline is varied, with several flat and slightly sloped roofs, some of which are topped with visible industrial equipment and vents. Large, industrial-style windows are distributed along the exterior, providing ample natural light to the interior workspaces. Loading dock doors and wide entryways are present, indicating areas for freight and vehicle access. The building's exterior is primarily composed of brick and corrugated metal, with some concrete elements visible at the base and around the loading areas. The overall design is utilitarian, with a focus on functionality and durability, characteristic of early-to-mid 20th-century industrial architecture.
description: A large, brick and metal factory building with multiple flat roofs, industrial windows, and visible rooftop machinery. Features include loading dock doors and a utilitarian, industrial design.
description_view_0: This view highlights the long side of the factory, showing several interconnected brick and metal structures, rooftop machinery, and a prominent loading dock area.
description_view_1: From this angle, the rear and side of the building are visible, revealing additional industrial windows, a large arched entryway, and more rooftop equipment.
description_view_2: This perspective emphasizes the opposite long side, with a clear view of the corrugated metal roof sections, multiple loading dock doors, and a secondary building segment.
description_view_3: The front-facing view displays the main entrance, a central staircase, and the arrangement of industrial windows and rooftop features.
